studies.title:
deprexis®24-NOW
deprexis®24-new online cognitive behavioral therapy with or without pharmacotherapy
studies.health_condition:
ICD-10-GM (translation):
F32 - Depressive episode
Healthy volunteers:
No Entry
studies.brief_summary: Aim of this clinical study of a medical device according to §23b MPG is to examine the application and effectiveness of the online-based psychotherapy program deprexis®24 for the therapeutic support of adult patients with depressive mood or depression, with or without comedication. Data are collected under routine conditions over an observation period of 3 months.
studies.intervention:
Arm 1:
Data of ambulatory patients with a depressive mood or depression who use deprexis®24 as an online psychotherapy program according to the current patient information should be documented over an observational period of 3 months under routine care.
The therapy program can be used by patients with or without antidepressive comedication.
Patients with or without concomitant diseases should be enrolled into this study. Requirement for the inclusion of patients into this study is, that the trea

classification.reason: The study involves data collection under routine conditions, which aligns with real-world evidence characteristics. There is no explicit mention of randomization or controlled comparison, and the study type and allocation are not specified. While there is a preliminary suggestion of RCT, the lack of clear evidence for randomization makes it more likely to be RWE.

studies.title: Digital health applications for older people with depressive disorders – pilot study
studies.health_condition:

ICD-10-GM (translation):

Healthy
studies.intervention:
Arm 1:
For the pilot study, people aged 60 and above with a mild or moderate depressive disorder will be recruited by three clinics (study centers) involved in the project. Participation in the study requires prior information and consent from the participants as well as ensuring eligibility for inclusion. classification.reason: The study title and details do not explicitly mention randomization or controlled trial design. The term 'pilot study' suggests an exploratory phase, which could be observational. Without clear evidence of randomization or interventional design, it is more conservative to classify this as RWE.
